name: Council of Europe

colors.symbolism: perfection, unity and the gold of the stars

description: A blue field bearing twelve five-pointed gold stars arranged in a circle.
history: The flag of the Council of Europe was designed by Arsène Heitz and adopted by the Committee of Ministers on 8 December 1955. Twelve stars represents perfection and completeness, not the number of member states. The European Union later adopted the same flag in 1985 (formally 1986).

features.symbolism: perfection, completeness and the union of European peoples — twelve being a traditional number of completeness, not a count of member states
